2604.19561.title: Detecting Data Contamination in Large Language Models
2604.19561.paper_summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) utilize large amounts of data for their training, some of which may come from copyrighted sources. Membership Inference Attacks (MIA) aim to detect those documents and whether they have been included in the training corpora of the LLMs. The black-box MIAs require a significant amount of data manipulation; therefore, their comparison is often challenging. We study state-of-the-art (SOTA) MIAs under the black-box assumptions and compare them to each other using a unified set of datasets to determine if any of them can reliably detect membership under SOTA LLMs. In addition, a new method, called the Familiarity Ranking, was developed to showcase a possible approach to black-box MIAs, thereby giving LLMs more freedom in their expression to understand their reasoning better. The results indicate that none of the methods are capable of reliably detecting membership in LLMs, as shown by an AUC-ROC of approximately 0.5 for all methods across several LLMs. The higher TPR and FPR for more advanced LLMs indicate higher reasoning and generalizing capabilities, showcasing the difficulty of detecting membership in LLMs using black-box MIAs.
2604.19561.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）在訓練過程中利用大量數據，其中一些可能來自受版權保護的來源。會員推斷攻擊（MIA）的目的是檢測這些文件以及它們是否已被納入LLMs的訓練語料庫。黑箱MIA需要大量的數據操作；因此，它們的比較往往具有挑戰性。我們在黑箱假設下研究最先進（SOTA）的MIA，並使用統一的數據集將它們彼此比較，以確定其中是否有任何方法能夠可靠地檢測SOTA LLMs下的會員。此外，開發了一種新方法，稱為熟悉度排名，以展示對黑箱MIA的一種可能方法，從而使LLMs在表達上有更多自由，以更好地理解其推理。結果表明，沒有任何方法能夠可靠地檢測LLMs中的會員，所有方法在多個LLMs中顯示的AUC-ROC約為0.5。更先進的LLMs的較高TPR和FPR表明其推理和概括能力更強，顯示出使用黑箱MIA檢測LLMs中的會員的困難。
2604.19561.author: Juliusz Janicki et.al.
2604.19561.authors: Juliusz Janicki, Savvas Chamezopoulos, Evangelos Kanoulas, Georgios Tsatsaronis
